title: Against unitary theories of music evolution

journal: Behavioral and Brain Sciences
link: https://doi.org/10.1017/s0140525x20001314
abstract: Savage et al. and Mehr et al. provide well-substantiated arguments that
  the evolution of musicality was shaped by adaptive functions of social bonding and
  credible signalling. However, they are too quick to dismiss byproduct explanations
  of music evolution, and to present their theories as complete unitary accounts of
  the phenomenon.
